title: Release Automation, npm Trusted Publishing, and Change Control · Automation Case: Ci Release Version Source Authority
summary: A release is triggered from an unreviewed tag, rebuilds after approval, publishes npm with a long-lived token, mixes changelog generation with mutation, and cannot prove package or image provenance. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ci release version source authority increment in multi-artifact release and publication policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Derive version, tag, changelog, release notes, source commit, and authorization from a protected reviewed release process.
Keep application deployment separate from public access and IAM mutation, with explicit ownership, review, audit, and recovery for each.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.
workspace: cicd
version-baseline:
  checkout: 7.0.0
  setup-node: 7.0.0
  upload-artifact: 7.0.1
  download-artifact: 8.0.1
  attest: 4.1.1
  node: 24-lts
  typescript-native: 7.0.2
  typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
  build-push-action: 7
  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-delivery-environment

steps.title: Predict Ci Release Version Source Authority · Theory Ci Release Version Source Authority
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory tag rebuild publishes npm and images with long-lived tokens, missing provenance, and one-person approval. Build once, promote immutable artifacts, use trusted publishing, verify contents, and separate duties. Competency probe: Derive version, tag, changelog, release notes, source commit, and authorization from a protected reviewed release process.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example tag rebuild publishes npm and images with long-lived tokens, missing provenance, and one-person approval. Build once, promote immutable artifacts, use trusted publishing, verify contents, and separate duties. Competency probe: Derive version, tag, changelog, release notes, source commit, and authorization from a protected reviewed release process. A defensible solution applies Ci Release Version Source Authority, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to multi-artifact release and publication policy.

content.code:
# Evidence: ci-worked-cd-releases-packages-change-control-5
  - competency: ci-worked-cd-releases-packages-change-control-5
    artifact: release-policy.yaml
    decision: worked cd releases packages change control 5
    environment: single-job ephemeral self-hosted runner in a segmented subnet
    fault-injection: expire the only retained test artifact during incident reconstruction
    retained-proof: accessible redacted timeline with owner expiry and regression control
    case-key: worked5
    change: |
      source: protected-reviewed-tag
      checks: required-at-source-sha
      build: once
      approval: promote-existing-artifact
      image: immutable-digest
      npm: trusted-publishing
      npm-minimum: 11.5.1
      node-minimum: 22.14.0
      id-token: release-job-only
      package-provenance: verified
      separation: author-reviewer-builder-approver-publisher
    verify: compare tag authority source checks artifact digest package contents trusted publisher identity provenance approval and published result
    failure: Reject tag-only authorization, per-environment rebuild, long-lived publish token, OIDC for private install, provenance as benign-code proof, or approval count as safety.
    repair: Protect source authority, build once, approve promotion, publish through exact OIDC identity, verify package provenance, and separate duties by risk.
content.caption: Runnable CI/CD workflow, artifact, release, and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A signed tag proves the tagged source passed current required checks. This fails the Ci Release Version Source Authority contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.value: npm Trusted Publishing and Package Provenance (npm trusted publishing with npm 11.5.1 or later, current 2026-07-14) — Controls OIDC publication, supported runners, workflow identity, token removal, staged publishing, package provenance, and limitations.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Derive version, tag, changelog, release notes, source commit, and authorization from a protected reviewed release process.
